name: This recipe was inspired by the Vietnamese vets who came over into Thailand on their breaks and
intro: have become popular all on their own in the Northeast, where this is a popular street food breakfast.

ingredients.item: shrimp, cleaned and minced or fi nely chopped
shallots). If using regular shallots, use ½ lb (225 g) or 3–4 large shallots, peeled and sliced
coarsely chopped dried chilies depending on how spicy you want the dish

method: Soak the glass vermicelli in water for half an hour. Then drain and set aside.
While the noodles are soaking, stir-fry the shrimp in unscented oil until pink. Remove and set aside.
Add more cooking oil and the shallots to the pan and stir-fry until the shallots turned opaque.
Pour 1 cup of the coconut milk into a pot, then season with the palm sugar, fi sh sauce and tama- rind juice. Mix and heat until boil- ing, stirring occasionally. Set aside and reserve for later use.
“Stir-fry” the drained glass vermicelli in the remaining 1 cup
